title: The Feast of All Saints
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver
## November 1, 2020 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

All Saints' Day falls on November 1st and is one of the principal feasts of the church year. The RCL readings for this Sunday would typically include Revelation 7:9-17 (the great multitude in white robes), 1 John 3:1-3 (we are God's children), and Matthew 5:1-12 (the Beatitudes), creating a thematic focus on the communion of saints and the blessed life. The church calendar places this in Ordinary Time following Pentecost, though All Saints' observances carry their own particular solemnity and theological weight regardless of season.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was preparing for its triennial General Convention, which had been postponed from summer 2021 due to COVID-19 and would eventually be held in July 2021 in Baltimore. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Robert Wright, was navigating pandemic protocols for worship and pastoral care, with many parishes operating under restricted gathering guidelines. No major diocesan leadership elections or significant controversies appear to have coincided specifically with this date.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in the final week before the November 3rd presidential election, with Colorado having emerged as a swing state receiving national attention. The city was experiencing early November weather patterns (typically cool and dry). The Capitol Hill neighborhood, home to Saint John's Cathedral, was seeing increased political activism and campaign signage throughout the week leading into Election Day.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The United States was five days away from the 2020 presidential election, with polling, campaign rallies, and election security concerns dominating national coverage. COVID-19 cases were rising sharply across the country heading into fall, with over 230,000 deaths reported nationally by this date. The Supreme Court was hearing arguments on the Affordable Care Act in a case that could overturn the law, argued just days before on October 28th.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The global COVID-19 pandemic had reached over 47 million confirmed cases worldwide with over 1.2 million deaths. Europe was experiencing a severe second wave of infections, with France, the UK, and other nations implementing new lockdowns in late October. The U.S. presidential election was drawing international attention and concern from allies about American democratic stability.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The national mood was anxious and polarized, with deep political division and election anxiety at a peak. Uncertainty about COVID-19's trajectory, economic impacts, and social unrest created a sense of instability and loss that would have made All Saints' Day meditations on death, memory, and the communion of saints particularly resonant. The cultural conversation was dominated by the election and pandemic; most Americans were experiencing genuine fear about the immediate future, making themes of eternal communion and transcendent perspective theologically significant.
